verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I TM protein resident in the ER membrane, where its luminal EF-hand senses Ca2+ store depletion and it oligomerizes at ER-PM junctions to gate Orai channels. Its dominant compartment is the ER, but a documented minority pool reaches the plasma membrane (PM-STIM1) with the N-terminal EF-SAM domain exposed extracellularly, and surface STIM1 has been detected by surface biotinylation and targeted by antibodies in functional studies. This qualifies as dual_localization — an ER-resident TM protein with a real, if minor, surface pool with extracellular N-terminus.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface STIM1 pool is small and context-dependent; whether it is robust/abundant enough for therapeutic targeting is unclear.
